Ferdinand (2017)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $84,410,380Details
International Box Office $223,081,265
Worldwide Box Office $307,491,645
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $19,577,200
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $15,739,545
Total $35,316,745
Further financial details...

Synopsis

After being mistaken for a dangerous beast, Ferdinand is captured and torn from his home. Determined to return to his family, he rallies a misfit team on the ultimate adventure. Ferdinand proves you can’t judge a bull by its cover.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$13,401,586 (15.9% of total gross)
Legs:6.30 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:27.5% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$111,000,000 (worldwide box office is 2.8 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,621 opening theaters/3,630 max. theaters, 5.9 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $111,914,767

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 15th, 2017 (Wide) by 20th Century Fox
International Releases: December 8th, 2017 (Wide), released as Bulius Ferdinandas (Lithuania)
December 15th, 2017 (Wide) (Australia)
December 15th, 2017 (Wide) (Indonesia)
December 15th, 2017 (Wide) (India)
December 15th, 2017 (Wide) (Italy)
Video Release: February 27th, 2018 by Fox Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG for rude humor, action and some thematic elements.
(Rating bulletin 2497 (Cert #51275), 10/11/2017)
Running Time: 107 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Animal Lead, Remake
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Shot in 3-D
Plot point: Bullfighting, Talking Animals
Subgenre: Family Adventure
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Digital Animation
Creative Type:Kids Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Blue Sky Studios, 20th Century Fox Animation, Davis Entertainment
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

After being mistaken for a dangerous beast, Ferdinand is captured and torn from his home. Determined to return to his family, he rallies a misfit team on the ultimate adventure. Ferdinand proves you can’t judge a bull by its cover.
